full_prompt: Design the end-beam and collision-post assembly of a Tier I commuter passenger rail car body per 49 CFR 238 Subpart C, meeting the 800,000 lbf buff load, 500,000 lbf vertical anti-telescoping requirement, 10 g collision-post design, and corner-post energy-absorbing design. The assembly spans the full car width 3.05 m (10 ft) and 2.6 m (8.5 ft) height, with two collision posts on the longitudinal centerline axes of the draft gear and two corner posts at the outer corners. Section envelope: end beam 250 x 200 mm box, collision posts 200 x 150 mm x 2.6 m tall, corner posts 150 x 150 mm x 2.6 m tall, all welded from A572 Grade 50 structural steel plate (yield 345 MPa, UTS 450 MPa, Young's modulus 200 GPa, density 7850 kg/m^3, Charpy minimum 20 J at -40 C per FRA guidance). Load cases per 49 CFR 238.203, 238.205, 238.207: LC1 buff load 800,000 lbf (3.56 MN) compressive along the line of the draft gear centers, distributed across the end beam and collision posts, carrier must sustain without permanent deformation; LC2 anti-telescoping vertical 500,000 lbf (2.22 MN) applied at anti-climber; LC3 collision post 300,000 lbf (1.33 MN) ultimate applied 18 inches (457 mm) above the top of the underframe at the collision post, must be sustained without failure; LC4 collision post intermediate 500,000 lbf (2.22 MN) at 30 inches (762 mm) above underframe; LC5 corner post 150,000 lbf (667 kN) ultimate applied 18 inches above underframe; LC6 corner post intermediate 300,000 lbf (1.33 MN) at 30 inches; LC7 fatigue spectrum per APTA PR-CS-S-034-99 revenue-service cycling. Pass/fail per 49 CFR 238.203/205/207 and AAR Manual Standard S-034: (R1) at LC1 buff (800,000 lbf), maximum stress in all primary members must remain below yield 345 MPa with no permanent deformation; (R2) at LC2 anti-telescoping 500,000 lbf, primary structure shall resist without failure - max stress <= UTS = 450 MPa; (R3) at LC3 collision post ultimate 300,000 lbf at 18 in, carrier must resist without failure (max stress <= UTS 450 MPa); (R4) at LC4 intermediate 500,000 lbf at 30 in, max stress <= yield 345 MPa; (R5) at LC5 corner post ultimate 150,000 lbf at 18 in <= UTS 450 MPa; (R6) at LC6 corner post intermediate 300,000 lbf at 30 in <= yield 345 MPa; (R7) fatigue Category B weld detail per AAR S-034 + AWS D15.1 for 300,000 cycles per year over 30-year revenue life; (R8) all weld procedures qualified to AWS D1.1 + FRA minimum impact (Charpy 20 J at -40 C); (R9) car body must not penetrate collision post 18-inch line by more than a defined deformation limit under LC3. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the end-beam/corner-post weldment with weld callouts; FEA report; fatigue analysis per AAR S-034; weld procedure specifications per AWS D1.1.
short_prompt:
Design the end-beam and collision-post assembly of a Tier I commuter passenger rail car body per 49 CFR 238 Subpart C, meeting the 800,000 lbf buff load, 500,000 lbf vertical anti-telescoping, 10 g collision-post design, and corner-post energy-absorbing design.

Geometry: assembly spans full car width 3.05 m (10 ft) and 2.6 m (8.5 ft) height; two collision posts on the longitudinal centerline axes of the draft gear; two corner posts at outer corners. Section envelope: end beam 250 x 200 mm box, collision posts 200 x 150 mm x 2.6 m, corner posts 150 x 150 mm x 2.6 m, all welded.

Material: A572 Grade 50 plate, Charpy minimum 20 J at -40 C per FRA guidance.

Load cases per 49 CFR 238.203/205/207.
- LC1 buff: 800,000 lbf (3.56 MN) compressive along draft-gear centers.
- LC2 anti-telescoping vertical: 500,000 lbf (2.22 MN) at anti-climber.
- LC3 collision post ultimate: 300,000 lbf (1.33 MN) at 18 inches (457 mm) above underframe.
- LC4 collision post intermediate: 500,000 lbf (2.22 MN) at 30 inches (762 mm).
- LC5 corner post ultimate: 150,000 lbf (667 kN) at 18 inches.
- LC6 corner post intermediate: 300,000 lbf (1.33 MN) at 30 inches.
- LC7 fatigue spectrum per APTA PR-CS-S-034-99.

Weld details per AWS D1.1 + AISC 360 Appendix 3 fatigue Category B for 300,000 cycles/year over 30-year revenue life (per AAR S-034). Weld procedures qualified to AWS D1.1 with FRA minimum Charpy 20 J at -40 C.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: AWS D1.1 + AAR S-034 Category weld fatigue check is a closed-form S-N based calculation.

prompt.title: FRA Tier I commuter rail car end-beam + collision/corner post assembly
prompt.brief: Design the end-beam + collision/corner posts of a Tier I commuter rail car per 49 CFR 238 Subpart C: 800,000 lbf buff, collision post 300,000 lbf, corner post 150,000 lbf, 30-year fatigue life.

geometric_constraints.collision_posts: 2 x 200 x 150 mm x 2.6 m tall
geometric_constraints.corner_posts: 2 x 150 x 150 mm x 2.6 m tall

boundary_conditions.car_body_underframe: end-beam assembly welded to underframe, considered fixed at underframe

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: AAR S-034 weld category B for 30-year revenue life
FRA guidance for Tier I cold climate

requirements.governing_standard: 49 CFR 238 Subpart C + APTA PR-CS-S-034-99 + AAR Manual Standard S-034 + AWS D1.1

analysis_types: linear static x6 + weld fatigue S-N check

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Static buff + collision + fatigue governs; CFR 238 does not mandate modal for car body ends.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal-cycle of welded assembly is secondary; Charpy substitutes for low-temperature toughness.

notes.numeric_source: 49 CFR 238 Subpart C specific loads: 800,000 lbf buff (238.203), 500,000 lbf anti-telescoping (238.205), 300,000 lbf collision post (238.211), 150,000 lbf corner post (238.213).
